text: The title compound 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1) has been synthesized from the reaction between 4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol and 4-(chloromethyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one. The hybrid compound 1 was characterized via element analysis of elemental data, IR, 1H and 13C APT NMR techniques. Additionally, density functional theory (DFT) at B3LYP level including 6–31 G (d) basis set was employed to determine the molecular structure, electronic structure and related features for 1 in the gas phase. The chemical reactivity descriptors such as the electric dipole moment and other related quantities like molecular electrostatic potential for the title compound 1 have also been elucidated via this computational tool. The ADMET properties for the title compound were determined using on line predilections, such as GI absorption, and human blood-brain barrier penetration for compound 1. On considering Toxicity Model Report, the compound 1 belonged to toxicity class 4 which indicated no concerns against different toxicity models. Molecular docking study revealed the potent interaction of compound 1 toward the selected targets for the treatment of SARS-CoV-2 by showing binding affinities within the range −39 to −9 kcal/mol. In addition, a strong interaction was realized toward all the SARS-CoV-2 proteins with maximum interaction energy with the Papain-like protease (PLpro) while it has the least interaction energy toward the Nucleocapsid protein (NP). Moreover, it is pertinent to mention here that molecular dynamics simulation further supported the findings obtained via docking studies, when studied via MD simulation for the most interacted SARS-CoV-2 proteins in order to corroborate and establish the stability of the docked poses between the target SARS-CoV-2 protein receptors and compound 1.
